brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (No, 8 years not 6) with valid piecewise reasoning; goes one level beyond surface by decomposing into two phases with explicit year-by-year tables, but does not explore second-order effects like non-linear growth variance or saturation; proceeds linearly without naming potential biases or reflecting on method choice mid-analysis; verifies by cross-checking analyst formula against correct piecewise model and showing the 2-year discrepancy, but no boundary-condition testing; states conclusions confidently without acknowledging linearity assumptions or confidence intervals around growth rates; implicitly contrasts analyst approach vs correct approach but does not evaluate alternative interpretations like mid-year threshold crossing or variable growth rates; every conclusion traces to explicit derivation with reproducible tables and labeled formulas; correctly identifies the 50% threshold as a phase boundary and applies different rules per regime without explicitly naming the anti-pattern it is avoiding; avoids all suppressed failure modes — no linear extrapolation, no regime blindness, no start-end-only thinking — and the structure actively counters the analyst's exact suppressed error; maps pre- and post-transition regimes and isolates the transition boundary at Year 4, following the core topology flow, though without explicit gate/loop/M-node structure visible in the response.
